summary: Reject bad M2 maps and bad content references
acceptance_criteria: Content compiler tests reject bad M2 map inputs, including malformed geometry, invalid district/settlement anchors, duplicate stable IDs, disconnected or unmarked isolated graph parts, and invalid route endpoint references.

Content compiler tests reject bad references across districts, settlements, regional seasonal curves, route definitions, localization keys, and manifest entries with structured schema errors containing paths and codes.
Bad-map and bad-reference fixtures are clearly marked as validation-only and cannot be mistaken for production or historical content.
Valid M2 world fixture output remains deterministic after the guard additions.
No runtime arbitrary code execution, object graph serialization, Node filesystem dependency inside sim-core, UI work, or product-scope expansion is introduced.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-CONTENT-GUARDS-001 and new bad-fixture manifests
notes: This task satisfies the Roadmap M2 exit requirement that the content compiler reject bad maps and bad references.
Keep compiler errors explicit and deterministic; do not defer map/reference failures to runtime boot.
